title: OTC: Type-Constrained Abductive Completion
summary: Successfully generated a publication-ready LaTeX paper with PDF compilation. The paper introduces Ontological Type-Constrained Abductive Completion (OTC), a neuro-symbolic pipeline that uses hierarchical SLD proof-trace analysis and ontological reasoning to constrain abductive fact generation. The paper includes: (1) Title, abstract, and 6 main sections (Introduction, Related Work, Methods, Experiments, Discussion, Conclusion); (2) All 4 figures from available_figures properly inserted at their FIGURE:figN marker locations - fig1 (OTC Pipeline Architecture, 21:9) placed as figure* after Contributions section, fig2 (Dataset Statistics, 21:9) placed as figure after table in Experiments, fig3 (Performance Comparison, 21:9) placed as figure* after Results table, fig4 (Hierarchical Analysis Accuracy, 21:9) placed as figure after Analysis section; (3) Bibliography with 11 references from references.bib, properly cited using natbib cite commands; (4) Compiled to 15-page PDF (paper.pdf, 5.6MB) with no compilation errors - only minor overfull warnings resolved by using path for long Prolog code snippets and xurl package for URL breaking; (5) Verification confirmed: grep -c 'includegraphics' = 4, matching 4 figure files in figures/ directory. The PDF was visually reviewed by converting to PNG images - all pages display correctly with proper figure placement, caption rendering, and citation resolution.
